Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The normative process has two main paths one for when inventory is sufficient and another when it is not

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The process starts with Receive order Then comes Check inventory If inventory is sufficient the steps are Authorize Payment Package items Ship order Close order If inventory is not sufficient it should be Cancel order then Close order

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Importantly certain steps cannot happen in a different order For example Authorize Payment must come after Check inventory and before Package items Also after Cancel order you cannot do Package Ship etc Close order must be the last step in both scenarios

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 1 The steps are Receive Check Authorize Package Ship Close That looks exactly like the sufficient inventory path So this should be okay No violations here

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2 Steps are Receive Check Package Authorize Ship Close The problem here is the order of Authorize Payment and Package items According to the rules Authorize should come before Package But in this case Package is done before Authorize That is a violation

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The norm says that payment authorization must be after checking inventory and before packaging So packaging before authorization is not allowed

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 3 Receive Check Authorize Cancel Ship Close Wait after Cancel order they Ship order That is a big no no The cancellation path should go to Close order immediately Shipping after cancellation is definitely a violation

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: If they did Check inventory and then decided to cancel they should not have authorized payment The description says that if inventory is insufficient the process must branch into cancellation So if they did Check inventory and then Cancel we can infer inventory was insufficient

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So in this case the Cancel order step should come right after Check inventory But here they did Authorize Payment before Cancel That is a violation Also after Cancel they should not Ship So this case has two violations

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 4 Receive Check Cancel Close That is the correct path for insufficient inventory No violations here

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 5 Receive Check Authorize Ship Close Where is the Package items step The norm says after Authorize Payment you have to Package items before Ship So in this case they authorized payment then shipped without packaging That is a violation

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The norm says Authorize Payment must occur after Check inventory and before Package items Package items must be after Authorize and before Ship Ship must be after Package and never after Cancel Close order is the final activity

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: How do we know the inventory was insufficient The event log does not explicitly state the outcome of Check inventory However since the case eventually Cancels we can infer that Check inventory determined it was insufficient Therefore the process should have gone Check Cancel
